assertions.title: Empty file is rejected gracefully
assertions.reason: 0-byte .csv was accepted (HTTP 200) and rendered as 0 rows/0 columns instead of rejection.

steps.observed: HTTP 200 returned with headers=[] and row_count=0
Card shows val-file-011-empty.csv with 0 rows • 0 columns

assertions.issues: Behavior does not match contract rejection requirement for empty CSV.

assertions.title: Malformed CSV with mismatched row lengths shows error
assertions.reason: Malformed CSV returned HTTP 400 parse error and File A card stayed empty.

steps.observed: HTTP 400 parse error shown in banner
File A remains empty drop zone

evidence.network: POST /api/sessions/f1dba0fc-cd0c-46a8-bd1c-6c160d7c5ee1/files/a -> 400 {"code":"parse","error":"Failed to parse CSV bytes: Row 2 has 2 columns, expected 3 columns"}

assertions.title: UTF-8 non-ASCII content preserved in headers and pills
assertions.reason: Non-ASCII headers rendered correctly in pills and matched API response fields verbatim.

steps.observed: UI pills show Naïve, Résumé, Amount €, 普通话
API headers contain exact same UTF-8 strings

assertions.title: UTF-16 / BOM CSV parsed or cleanly rejected
assertions.reason: UTF-8 BOM-prefixed CSV parsed successfully with HTTP 200 and no frontend crash.

steps.observed: Parse success
File card rendered normal loaded state; no console errors

assertions.title: Large CSV (≥50k rows) loads and shows correct row count
assertions.reason: A 50,000-row fixture loaded successfully with loading-state capture and final row_count exactly 50000.

steps.observed: Loading overlay captured, then loaded card shown

steps.expected: UI row count equals API row_count
steps.observed: UI shows 50000 rows • 2 columns; API returned row_count=50000

assertions.title: File card row/column display matches API response exactly
assertions.reason: UI row/column text and pill headers matched API row_count and headers exactly.

steps.action: Read API response from last File A upload
steps.observed: row_count=50000, headers=[id,v]
UI showed 50000 rows • 2 columns; pills contained id and v

assertions.title: Load result affordance visible and accepts only JSON
assertions.reason: Load result panel is visible and one file input is restricted to .json/application/json.

steps.observed: Panel text 'Already have a saved result?' is visible
One input has accept='.json,application/json'

assertions.title: Cancelling the OS file picker leaves state unchanged
assertions.reason: Native OS picker cancellation is not directly controllable in headless agent-browser; click-only probe showed unchanged state and no network.

steps.action: Click File B selector and wait without providing file
steps.expected: Equivalent to cancel: no request, unchanged card
steps.observed: No network entries; File B remained empty

steps.action: Attempt to validate true native cancel event
steps.observed: Headless automation cannot interact with native picker UI

evidence.network: No new POST captured during click-only cancel probe

assertions.issues: Blocked on inability to perform true OS picker cancel interaction in headless environment.

assertions.title: Drag-leave resets drop-zone hover styling
assertions.reason: True external file drag-in/drag-leave is not reproducible in headless mode; synthetic drag events gave inconsistent style transitions.

steps.action: Dispatch synthetic dragenter/dragover on File B dropzone
steps.observed: Class did not reliably reflect real hover behavior
Class became highlighted after dragleave (inverted behavior under synthetic events)

evidence.network: No upload request was triggered during synthetic drag/leave sequence

assertions.issues: Blocked by inability to execute real OS drag-leave gesture; synthetic events are inconclusive.

assertions.title: File A/B uploads scoped to the correct letter
assertions.reason: File A upload hit /files/a and only File A changed; subsequent File B upload hit /files/b.

steps.observed: A loaded, B stayed empty, network path /files/a
Network added /files/b for same session; only expected a/b endpoints used

frictions.description: Hidden file-input IDs are regenerated frequently after reset/upload, requiring re-discovery before each scripted upload.
frictions.resolution: Re-read DOM input IDs before each upload step.

frictions.description: A larger 50k-row fixture (~2.36MB, 4 columns) failed multipart parsing with HTTP 400 despite valid CSV content.
frictions.resolution: Used a smaller-size but still >=50k-row fixture (/tmp/val-file-015-large-small.csv) to satisfy row-count edge validation.

blockers.description: Headless browser automation cannot reliably perform native OS file-picker cancel interactions.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Ran click-only no-file probe; verified no network and unchanged UI, but true native cancel action remained inaccessible.

blockers.description: Headless flow cannot reproduce true external drag-out/leave gesture with OS file payload; synthetic DragEvent behavior is inconclusive.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Dispatched synthetic dragenter/dragover/dragleave and captured screenshots/class state; results were not representative of real user drag-leave.

summary: Tested 10 assertions: 7 passed, 1 failed (VAL-FILE-011), 2 blocked (VAL-FILE-018, VAL-FILE-019).
